user_query:
I’m starting to apply TBM thinking to our internal app spend, and I want a small, re-runnable reporting job I can schedule weekly. Please implement a script or tool that:

- Scans input/costs/ for CSV files named cost_YYYY-MM.csv and treats each file as that month’s data.
- Extracts a service-to-capability mapping from input/mappings/service_to_capability.json.
- Extracts capability priority ranks by parsing the HTML table with id="capabilities" in input/tbm_taxonomy.html (read the table as two columns: Capability and PriorityRank; PriorityRank is an integer where 1 is highest priority).
- Filters each month’s records to env == "prod" and status == "Active".
- Aggregates spend by month and service (sum the cost), then attaches the mapped capability and the capability’s PriorityRank from the HTML. If a capability is not found in the HTML table, leave its rank blank.
- For each processed month, writes a CSV to output/reports/monthly/<YYYY-MM>_aggregated.csv with the header columns exactly: month,service,capability,capability_priority_rank,total_cost. Sort each month’s file by total_cost descending.
- Also writes a single output/reports/top_services_overall.csv containing the top 5 services by summed total_cost across all months (from the scanned files), with columns: service,capability,total_cost,rank. Break ties by lower capability_priority_rank first, then by service name ascending.
- Writes a manifest at output/manifest/processed_files.json listing, for each input cost CSV that was included, the file_path, file_size_bytes, modified_time_iso, and record_count_included (number of rows that passed the filter) so I can verify exactly what was processed.
- Produces a scheduling stub at output/schedule/weekly_cron.txt containing a single cron line that would run your reporting script every Monday at 07:00 local time, sending stdout and stderr to output/logs/weekly_run.log (append is fine). The command should reference a script placed under scripts/ (you choose the language and filename) that executes the above pipeline. Do not install the cron entry—just write the one-line example.

Please make the job idempotent: running it multiple times with the same inputs should deterministically overwrite the monthly CSVs, top_services_overall.csv, and the manifest with the same results. Use only the provided inputs under input/ and write outputs only under output/ as specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
